p.strong: The Problem: Owner and Thief Race for the Last Item

div.p: Colors: 🔴 Red = contention/danger 🟢 Green = success/resolution 🟡 Yellow = caution/waiting Symbols: ✅ CAS success ❌ CAS failure (retry) 🔥 High contention

g.text: return None; // Lost to owner/other thief

ol.li: If top == expected, sets top = new
Returns success/failure Key property: Only ONE thread's CAS succeeds. Hardware guarantees atomicity.

text.tspan: // Task we read is "leaked" but OK
// Task we read is "leaked" but OK

ol.li: CAS failure means someone else took an item
Try again with fresh state Contention is temporary: After a few retries, either we succeed or queue is empty.

div.p: Round-robin stealing creates hotspots: All idle workers → steal from worker 0 All hit same top atomic → cache line bouncing Serialized CAS operations → poor scaling
Random stealing distributes load: Each thief picks random victim Contention spreads across all queues CAS operations proceed in parallel

g.text: Speed | < 10 cycles | Called on every steal
Per-thread | No sharing | Avoid cache contention
Crypto quality | NOT needed | Just load balancing

p.strong: Without proper ordering, races corrupt data:

ol.li: Thief reads top before owner writes bottom
Thief tries to steal non-existent item

ul.li: All subsequent reads see latest values

g.text: push: write task | Relaxed | Local, not published
push: fence before bottom | Release | Task visible before index
push: store bottom | Relaxed | Publish new bottom
pop: store bottom-1 | Relaxed | Tentative reserve
pop: fence before top | SeqCst | See thief's updates
steal: load top | Acquire | See owner's updates
steal: CAS top | Release | Publish our claim

ul.li: Losers retry with fresh state 2. Random stealing reduces contention
Each queue's top is a potential hotspot
Random selection minimizes collisions 3. Memory ordering is critical
Wrong ordering = subtle, rare bugs 4. Retry is cheap
CAS failure is rare in practice

g.text: Uncontended pop | 5-15 | Fast path, mostly local
Cache miss on top | 100-200 | Cross-core access
Worker 0 | W1, W2, W3, W4 | 🔥🔥🔥 HIGH
